exemplar.title: Optimizing the Profits of a Restaurant Business using their Profit

detailedExemplarData.title: Optimizing the Profits of a Restaurant Business using their Profit

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear title and concise subtitle focusing on profit context
Well-organized with a clear introduction, body (modelling and optimisation), and conclusion
Tables and figures are embedded logically to support the narrative
weaknesses: Frequent descriptive digressions and repeated explanations interrupt the logical flow
Abrupt transitions between key mathematical steps require reader inference
Lengthy background narrative and theoretical quotations inflate word count without advancing mathematics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates some coherence (Band 2) due to interruptions in narrative flow, but shows solid organisation (Band 3) with clear sections and sub-headings. Content is mostly relevant but not concise (Band 2). Averaging these bands and weighing coherence and relevance appropriately yields a holistic score of 2.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (data tables, regression plots, derivative graph)
Side-by-side comparison of cubic and quartic models aids conceptual understanding
Accurate computation and graphical presentation of the derivative function
weaknesses: Inconsistent mathematical notation (misplaced commas in numbers, explicit xâ± notation, misordered polynomial terms)
Missing figure numbers and inconsistent axis labels impede easy cross-referencing
Key logical steps (e.g., merging polynomials, verifying uniqueness of roots) are sketched rather than fully justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Notation is sometimes inconsistent (Band 2), but multiple forms of representation are used effectively (Band 3) and overall structure and clarity are good (borderline Band 3). Given the strength of the visual and comparative communication despite occasional lapses, a holistic score of 3 accurately reflects performance.

strengths: Use of a family restaurant context shows significant independent thinking
Narrative framing and selection of business-specific constraints demonstrate a clear personal approach
Forward-looking reflection on expanding data span and methodological improvements indicates initiative
weaknesses: Creativity is limited to polynomial degree selection without exploring alternative optimisation techniques
Personal narrative can be overly detailed relative to its mathematical relevance
Novelty in methodological approach beyond standard steps is minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant personal decision-making and a clear personal approach (Bands 2â2), but only some creativity and initiative (Band 1). On balance, these features justify a final score of 2 for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Thoughtful analysis of RÂ² values and clear recognition of over-fitting
Clear evaluation of model strengths, limitations, and suggestions for future improvements
Effective reflection on the unique maximum and rationale for model selection
weaknesses: Reflection is often brief and mostly descriptive, limiting its depth
Critical discussion of complexity trade-offs and model interpretability is insufficient
Justification of software choice and data-handling methods is not addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Depth of reflection is limited (Band 1), but there is thoughtful analysis (Band 2) and clear evaluation of outcomes (Band 2). Overall performance aligns with a holistic score of 2 for Reflection.

strengths: Relevant and appropriate use of regression analysis and calculus (profit function and derivatives) throughout
Mathematical methods are fully commensurate with SL course level and applied correctly
Accurate derivative computations matched by clear graphical representations demonstrate solid understanding
weaknesses: Incorrect statement about maximum number of roots for a quartic indicates a conceptual slip
Minor notation inaccuracies and rounding conventions affect precision
Limited discussion of sensitivity or higher-precision error analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematics is relevant and effectively applied (Band 4), fully commensurate with course level (Band 4), and demonstrates good understanding and accuracy with only minor errors (Band 4). Therefore, a score of 4 is justified for Use of Mathematics (SL).

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations of why higher-degree polynomials increase RÂ² and how to locate a maximum with the second derivative show a logical thread, but steps such as merging the revenue and cost polynomials or verifying only one real root are sketched rather than fully justified.  Hence structure and clarity are SOMEWHAT satisfactory but not consistently rigorous.

Derivatives are computed accurately, graphs match equations, and algebraic subtraction of polynomials is sensible, although minor notation slips persist.  Overall understanding is GOOD and mostly correct, warranting this band rather than higher, expert-level precision.

The student supplies a table of raw data, several regression plots and a graph of the derivative, combining symbolic functions, numerical summaries and visual displays.  While axes labels are occasionally abbreviated, the representations are generally EFFECTIVE in supporting discussion.

The overall narrative moves from introduction, data display, modelling, optimisation and conclusion, but the argument is frequently interrupted by long descriptive digressions and several sections repeat earlier explanations.  Links between successive mathematical steps (e.g., from selecting the septic model to forming the profit function) are present but occasionally abrupt, requiring the reader to infer missing justification.  Hence the exploration demonstrates SOME coherence rather than sustained logical development.
Beyond trying successive polynomial degrees, there is limited novelty.  The work does not extend to alternative optimisation techniques or sensitivity testing, so evidence of CREATIVITY is only SOME.
The student interprets RÂ² values, discusses over-fitting and recognises that only one critical point leads to a maximum.  This shows THOUGHTFUL, though not deep, analysis of results and process.
Strengths and limitations of the model are identified (data span, other factors).  The student notes future improvements, giving a CLEAR if not comprehensive evaluation of outcomes.
Reflection appears mainly in the conclusion, acknowledging time-span limits and potential over-reliance on a single yearâs data.  These comments are BRIEF and descriptive, providing LIMITED depth.

Standard notation is used (P(x), R(x), C(x), derivatives) but with frequent inconsistencies: commas instead of decimal points, x^1 written explicitly, mis-placed large commas in coefficients, and an erroneous quartic definition.  These errors impede fluency yet appropriate language is still evident in many places, justifying SOME appropriate use.

The narrative is framed around the studentâs intention to help the parentsâ business; selection of meal composition, cost estimates and business-specific constraints indicate a CLEAR personal approach rather than a generic exercise.

Considerable background narrative about family history and restaurant descriptions, as well as lengthy quotations of regression theory, inflate the word-count without advancing the mathematics.  Nevertheless most content still relates to the stated aim, so the work is MOSTLY relevant but not concise.

Regression analysis, construction of profit and derivative functions, and use of first/second derivative tests are all RELEVANT and applied to the aim.  The mathematics is appropriate and goes beyond trivial treatment, though not exhaustive.

Choosing the family restaurant context, collecting twelve months of data and deciding to compare several regression models illustrate SIGNIFICANT personal decision-making beyond textbook procedures, though much of the analysis closely follows standard optimisation steps.

A clear introduction, body (exploration) and evaluation section are provided, and sub-headings guide the reader through data, modelling and optimisation.  Tables and figures are embedded in sensible places.  Although occasional formatting issues occur (broken sentences, repeated captions), the overall structure is easy to follow and therefore classifies as WELL ORGANISED.
Polynomial regression, profit optimisation and calculus are firmly within SL course level and used correctly in most cases; the work is therefore FULLY commensurate with syllabus expectations.

annotations.comment: The prose describing month-by-month outputs is detailed, but unit labels (e.g., âNumber of outputsâ) in Table 1.1 are ambiguous. Clarify units.
The awareness of over-fitting solidly demonstrates reflection on modeling choices.
The explanation of cost, revenue, and profit integrates real business context but lacks clear linkage to the stated aim. Reinforce connections to optimization goal.
The introduction to using calculus and statistics, including $$P(x)=R(x)-C(x)$$, clearly establishes the mathematical foundation of the exploration.
Sentence fragment at the top of the page interrupts narrative flow. Revisit formatting to ensure coherence.
The aim is clearly stated and the equation $$P(x)=R(x)-C(x)$$ is introduced effectively. Display the equation separately for emphasis.
The cost breakdown mixes narrative and calculation. Consider tabulating component costs to improve clarity and conciseness.
Axes mapping of units and revenue is described clearly, reinforcing the validity of subsequent regression plots.
Figure 1.1âs caption is precise, but axis labels on the regression plot are abbreviated. Use full variable names for consistency.
The title is clear and sets the context, but consider refining it to avoid redundancy and improve precision (e.g., remove âusing their Profitâ).
The concise subtitle âtheir Profitâ succinctly indicates the focus on profit calculation, reinforcing the explorationâs aim.
The rich family narrative effectively conveys personal motivation but is excessively detailed. Streamline to focus on mathematical relevance.
The profit expansion step is comprehensive. To streamline, combine like terms and eliminate zero coefficients immediately.
When removing parentheses, cancelling zero terms would improve conciseness and reduce cognitive load.
Table 1.1 is well structured, but axis labels abbreviate abbreviations inconsistently (e.g., PHP vs. âPâ). Standardize notation.
The rationale for choosing an online calculator is not discussed. Include a brief justification of software and data handling methods.
Discussion of improved $$r^2$$ from 0.3157 to 0.781 is solid; add a brief remark on potential over-fitting at higher degrees.
The explanation of quartic regression flexibilities is clear and shows appropriate use of $$	ext{degree}=4$$ modelling.
You recognize over-fitting risk for higher-degree models. To deepen reflection, discuss when increasing degree yields negligible gains.
The interpretation of $$r^2=0.3157$$ is accurate. To deepen reflection, discuss implications for business forecasting under low explanatory power.
Acknowledging the limitation of linear fit and pivoting to polynomial regression shows critical awareness of model selection.
Two regression plots are shown but lack figure numbers in the caption. Label each clearly to aid cross-reference.
General form $$f(x)=ax^4 + b + cx^2 + dx + e$$ misplaces the $bx^3$ term. Correct notation to maintain mathematical accuracy.
Statement that quartic has âat most 2 rootsâ is incorrect; a degree-4 polynomial can have up to 4 real or complex roots.
The side-by-side display of cubic and quartic forms is well done, aiding comparison of polynomial structures.
Cubic equation coefficient â-8.863.6096â shows misplaced separators. Ensure coefficients are clearly notated as $$-8.8636096$$.
Septic cost model selection relies solely on $$r^2$$; reflect on interpretability and potential overfitting in cost context.
The visual comparison of septic fit in Figure 1.1 is very effective in demonstrating improved $$r^2$$.
Rationale for choosing septic model focuses on $$r^2$$ maximization only. Discuss trade-off with model complexity.
Septic regression equation uses inconsistent comma separators within coefficients, causing ambiguity. Standardize numeric formatting.
The choice to exclude exponential models is well reasoned by non-constant change in data, demonstrating appropriate model selection.
Transition to cost modelling is logical; consider previewing key cost drivers to frame the regression choice.
Cost regression figures are well integrated; adding residual plots would strengthen communication of fit quality.
Including nearly zero coefficients for a septic model shows recognition of structural completeness.
Derivative computation is accurate: $$P'(x)=0.002x^3-7.488x^2+14567.8418x-11702541.5853\
Stating the root at $x=1490$ is correct; add a second-derivative check to confirm a maximum point.
The conclusion clearly states the optimal output and reflects on the single-root scenario effectively.
References are varied but URLs in body text disrupt flow. Format web links in a separate appendix or endnote.
Final profit equation is correct but rounding at four decimals may obscure sensitivity. Consider higher precision.
The optimisation procedure is described clearly; include a brief proof of existence of exactly one real critical point.
The derivative graph is effectively presented, visually supporting root identification and maximizing clarity.
Introduce $$P''(x)=0.006x^2-14.976x+14567.8418$$ but apply it explicitly to check concavity at $x=1490$.
Evaluation recognizes data-span limitations. To enhance reflection, consider a sensitivity analysis on time-series length.
Reflection on expanding data span and adding mathematical methods shows forward planning and personal initiative.
Citing Investopedia and online calculators shows breadth but include peer-reviewed sources to strengthen academic rigor.
